name: Alvin Kamara
headline: Father Time arrives: efficiency collapse drops Kamara to dart-throw
body:
The 2025 tape is alarming. Kamara managed just 3.6 yards per carry and a single rushing score across 11 games, posting a brutal -0.238 EPA per play and only 67.7 fantasy points to finish RB42. Both the explosion and the contact balance evaporated: 1.44 yards after contact per attempt, a 7.6% explosive-run rate, and zero runs of 20-plus. The receiving safety net that defined his value cratered too, with target share falling to 6.9% (down from 17.2% in 2024) and just 0.70 yards per route run. At age 30, this looks like decline rather than noise.

The context is equally grim. He projects as a backup behind a younger lead back, with his snap share already sliding to 63.7%. Drafters are treating him accordingly, pushing him toward an RB56 price near pick 183. There is no health excuse here, only a fading skill set. Leave him on the board until the final rounds and only as desperation depth.
